[推荐一个基于 Spring Boot 4.0 + Java 21 + Spring AI 2.0 的大模型项目！ - 知乎](https://zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/2001430705301890269)\n3. [nacos-spring-ai-alibaba-example/a2a-example/README.md at main · nacos-group/nacos-spring-ai-alibaba-example · GitHub](https://github.com/nacos-group/nacos-spring-ai-alibaba-example/blob/main/a2a-example/README.md)\n4. [Upgrade Notes :: Spring AI Reference](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/upgrade-notes.html)\n5. [Tool Calling :: Spring AI Reference](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/tools.html)\n\n在 Spring AI 2.0 中,获取 Token Usage 的官方推荐方式是通过 &#96;ChatResponse&#96; 对象中的 &#96;Usage&#96; 元数据 [4, 9]。\n\n### 获取方式\n调用 &#96;ChatModel&#96; 或 &#96;ChatClient&#96; 后,从返回的 &#96;ChatResponse&#96; 中获取 &#96;Usage&#96; 对象 [4, 9]:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;java\nChatResponse response = chatModel.call(new Prompt(\"你的问题\"));\n\n// 获取 Usage 对象\nUsage usage = response.getMetadata().getUsage();\n\n// 获取具体 Token 数值\nInteger promptTokens = usage.getPromptTokens();\nInteger completionTokens = usage.getCompletionTokens();\nInteger totalTokens = usage.getTotalTokens();\n\nSystem.out.println(\"Input Tokens: \" + promptTokens);\nSystem.out.println(\"Output Tokens: \" + completionTokens);\nSystem.out.println(\"Total Tokens: \" + totalTokens);\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 关键点说明\n1. **API 变更**:在 Spring AI 1.0.0-M6 及后续版本中,&#96;Usage&#96; 接口的方法已统一为 &#96;getPromptTokens()&#96;、&#96;getCompletionTokens()&#96; 和 &#96;getTotalTokens()&#96; [4]。\n2. **数据类型**:&#96;DefaultUsage&#96; 实现中的 Token 计数已从 &#96;Long&#96; 变更为 &#96;Integer&#96; [4]。\n3. **可观测性集成**:Spring AI 2.0 提供了内置的可观测性支持,可以通过 Actuator 端点 &#96;/actuator/metrics/gen_ai.client.token.usage&#96; 实时监控 Token 消耗,无需手动解析每个响应 [9]。\n4. **流式响应**:如果使用流式调用(Streaming),需在配置中开启 &#96;spring.ai.openai.chat.options.stream-usage=true&#96;,以便在最后一个 Chunk 中获取完整的 Usage 统计信息 [1]。\n\nReferences:\n[1] https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/openai-chat.html\n    Title: OpenAI Chat :: Spring AI Reference\n    Relevance: This page lists configuration properties for Spring AI's OpenAI integration, including options for specifying organization IDs, project IDs, and various chat model parameters. However, it does not directly show an API for retrieving token usage.\n[2] https://zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/2001430705301890269\n    Title: 推荐一个基于 Spring Boot 4.0 + Java 21 + Spring AI 2.0 的大模型项目！ - 知乎\n    Relevance: This page discusses a large model project built with Spring Boot 4.0, Java 21, and Spring AI 2.0. While it mentions features like API rate limiting and RAG knowledge base Q\u0026A, it does not directly provide the official API for obtaining token usage in Spring AI 2.0.\n[3] https://github.com/nacos-group/nacos-spring-ai-alibaba-example/blob/main/a2a-example/README.md\n    Title: nacos-spring-ai-alibaba-example/a2a-example/README.md at main · nacos-group/nacos-spring-ai-alibaba-example · GitHub\n    Relevance: 该页面是关于 Spring AI Alibaba 和 Nacos 的示例项目,其中提到了阿里云百炼的 API-KEY,并说明了首次开通会提供免费额度。然而,页面内容并未直接展示如何获取 Spring AI 2.0 的 token usage 的官方 API 写法。\n[4] https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/upgrade-notes.html\n    Title: Upgrade Notes :: Spring AI Reference\n    Relevance: This page discusses breaking changes and upgrades for Spring AI, including changes to &#96;ChatClient&#96; and &#96;VectorStoreChatMemoryAdvisor&#96;. It does not directly provide the official API for retrieving token usage in Spring AI 2.0.\n[5] https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/tools.html\n    Title: Tool Calling :: Spring AI Reference\n    Relevance: This page discusses Spring AI's tool calling capabilities, including how to define and provide tools to a model using &#96;ChatClient&#96; and &#96;@Tool&#96; annotations. However, it does not directly address how to retrieve token usage information.\n[6] https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/bedrock-converse.html\n    Title: Bedrock Converse API :: Spring AI Reference\n    Relevance: This page discusses Spring AI's integration with Amazon Bedrock's Converse API, which is recommended for chat conversations. While it details configuration and runtime options for the Converse API, it does not explicitly mention how to retrieve token usage information.\n[7] https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/retrieval-augmented-generation.html\n    Title: Retrieval Augmented Generation :: Spring AI Reference\n    Relevance: This page discusses Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) in Spring AI and introduces the &#96;Advisor&#96; API, including &#96;QuestionAnswerAdvisor&#96; and &#96;RetrievalAugmentationAdvisor&#96;. [Chat Client API :: Spring AI Reference](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chatclient.html)\n4. [万字长文：Oh My OpenCode 深度架构分析报告- - yzddMr6](https://yzddmr6.com/posts/oh-my-opencode-analyse/)\n5. [A Guide to Spring AI Advisors | Baeldung](https://www.baeldung.com/spring-ai-advisors)\n\nSpring AI provides two primary ways to handle logging and token tracking:\n\n### 1. Built-in Advisor for Logging\nThe &#96;SimpleLoggerAdvisor&#96; is the standard advisor for logging request and response data [3].\n* **Usage**: Add it to your &#96;ChatClient&#96; advisor chain [3].\n* **Configuration**: Set the logging level to &#96;DEBUG&#96; for the package &#96;org.springframework.ai.chat.client.advisor&#96; [3].\n* **Customization**: You can provide custom &#96;Function&#96; implementations to the &#96;SimpleLoggerAdvisor&#96; constructor to format the logged &#96;AdvisedRequest&#96; and &#96;ChatResponse&#96; data [3].\n\n### 2. Observability for Token Tracking\nFor production-grade monitoring, Spring AI integrates with **Spring Boot Actuator** to provide metrics and tracing [10].\n* **Metrics**: Use the &#96;gen_ai.client.token.usage&#96; metric to track input, output, and total tokens [10].\n* **Configuration**: Enable observability by adding the &#96;spring-boot-starter-actuator&#96; dependency [10].\n* **Logging Content**: You can enable logging of prompts and completions via properties in &#96;application.properties&#96; or &#96;application.yaml&#96; [10]:\n    * &#96;spring.ai.chat.observations.log-prompt=true&#96;\n    * &#96;spring.ai.chat.observations.log-completion=true&#96;\n\n### 3. Manual Access via &#96;ChatResponse&#96;\nIf you need to record tokens programmatically within your application logic, you can access the &#96;Usage&#96; metadata directly from the &#96;ChatResponse&#96; object [2, 3]:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;java\nChatResponse response = chatClient.prompt(\"Your prompt\")\n        .call()\n        .chatResponse();\n\nUsage usage = response.getMetadata().getUsage();\nSystem.out.println(\"Prompt Tokens: \" + usage.getPromptTokens());\nSystem.out.println(\"Completion Tokens: \" + usage.getCompletionTokens());\nSystem.out.println(\"Total Tokens: \" + usage.getTotalTokens());\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nFor model-specific details (like cached or reasoning tokens), you can cast &#96;usage.getNativeUsage()&#96; to the provider-specific implementation (e.g., &#96;org.springframework.ai.openai.api.OpenAiApi.Usage&#96;) [2].\n\nReferences:\n[1] https://blog.csdn.net/2402_84916296/article/details/151297212\n    Title: 【Spring AI】第二弹：Prompt 优化技巧、AI 应用开发\n    Relevance: 该页面解释了 Token 是大模型处理文本的基本单位,模型的输入和输出都按 Token 计算,并且 Token 数量会影响成本和输出速度。这与用户查询中关于统一记录输入输出和 Token 的 Advisor 用法相关。\n[2] https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/usage-handling.html\n    Title: Using Chat/Embedding Response Usage :: Spring AI Reference\n    Relevance: 该页面提供了 Spring AI 中 &#96;Usage&#96; 接口的增强功能,允许统一记录输入输出和 token。它展示了如何使用 &#96;getNativeUsage()&#96; 方法来访问模型特定的原生 token 使用情况,并提供了使用 &#96;ChatModel&#96; 和 &#96;ChatClient&#96; 的具体代码示例。\n[3] https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chatclient.html\n    Title: Chat Client API :: Spring AI Reference\n    Relevance: The webpage discusses the &#96;ChatClient&#96; API in Spring AI, which is relevant to your query about unified recording of input/output and tokens for Advisor usage. It specifically mentions \"Advisors\" and \"Advisor Configuration in ChatClient\" as sections, and the \"ChatClient Responses\" section explains that &#96;ChatResponse&#96; includes metadata about token usage.\n[4] https://yzddmr6.com/posts/oh-my-opencode-analyse/\n    Title: 万字长文：Oh My OpenCode 深度架构分析报告- - yzddMr6\n    Relevance: The webpage discusses a method to handle large plans that exceed LLM output token limits by first writing a skeleton and then using \"Edit\" to append tasks in batches. This directly addresses the user's query about managing LLM output token limitations for unified record input/output and tokens.\n[5] https://www.baeldung.com/spring-ai-advisors\n    Title: A Guide to Spring AI Advisors | Baeldung\n    Relevance: This page describes Spring AI Advisors, which act as interceptors for requests and responses in AI applications. It specifically details &#96;MessageChatMemoryAdvisor&#96; for managing chat history, which directly relates to the user's query about unified input/output and token usage for advisors.\n[6] https://www.cnblogs.com/ljbguanli/p/19123657\n    Title: 【SpringAI】第四弹：深入解析 Rag 检索增强工作流程、最佳实践和调优 - 详解 - ljbguanli - 博客园\n    Relevance: 该网页介绍了 Spring AI 中用于处理文档的 &#96;DocumentTransformer&#96; 接口,特别是 &#96;TokenTextSplitter&#96; 的用法,它能够根据 token 数量分割文本。此外,还提到了 &#96;VectorStore&#96; 接口及其 &#96;similaritySearch&#96; 方法,以及如何使用 &#96;SearchRequest&#96; 构建搜索请求,并解释了 &#96;TokenCountBatchingStrategy&#96; 如何通过估算 token 数来控制批次大小,这与用户查询的“统一记录输入输出和 token”的 Advisor 用法相关。\n[7] https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/discussions/450\n    Title: Log requests and responses to Open AI · spring-projects/spring-ai · Discussion #450 · GitHub\n    Relevance: This GitHub issue discusses logging requests and responses to OpenAI, which is relevant to managing input/output and tokens for an Advisor. {
  "markdown": "Using Chat/Embedding Response Usage :: Spring AI Reference\n\n# Using Chat/Embedding Response Usage\n\n## Overview\n\nSpring AI has enhanced its Model Usage handling by introducing&#96;getNativeUsage()&#96; method in the Usage interface and providing a&#96;DefaultUsage&#96; implementation. This change simplifies how different AI models can track and report their usage metrics while maintaining consistency across the framework.\n\n## Key Changes\n\n### Usage Interface Enhancement\n\nThe&#96;Usage&#96; interface now includes a new method:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nObject getNativeUsage();\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThis method allows access to the model-specific native usage data, enabling more detailed usage tracking when needed.\n\n### Using with ChatModel\n\nHere’s a complete example showing how to track usage with OpenAI’s ChatModel:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@SpringBootConfiguration\npublic class Configuration {\n\n        @Bean\n        public OpenAiApi chatCompletionApi() {\n            return OpenAiApi.builder()\n                .apiKey(System.getenv(\"OPENAI_API_KEY\"))\n                .build();\n        }\n\n        @Bean\n        public OpenAiChatModel openAiClient(OpenAiApi openAiApi) {\n            return OpenAiChatModel.builder()\n                .openAiApi(openAiApi)\n                .build();\n        }\n\n    }\n\n@Service\npublic class ChatService {\n\n    private final OpenAiChatModel chatModel;\n\n    public ChatService(OpenAiChatModel chatModel) {\n        this.chatModel = chatModel;\n    }\n\n    public void demonstrateUsage() {\n        // Create a chat prompt\n        Prompt prompt = new Prompt(\"What is the weather like today?\");\n\n        // Get the chat response\n        ChatResponse response = this.chatModel.call(prompt);\n\n        // Access the usage information\n        Usage usage = response.getMetadata().getUsage();\n\n        // Get standard usage metrics\n        System.out.println(\"Prompt Tokens: \" + usage.getPromptTokens());\n        System.out.println(\"Completion Tokens: \" + usage.getCompletionTokens());\n        System.out.println(\"Total Tokens: \" + usage.getTotalTokens());\n\n        // Access native OpenAI usage data with detailed token information\n        if (usage.getNativeUsage() instanceof org.springframework.ai.openai.api.OpenAiApi.Usage) {\n            org.springframework.ai.openai.api.OpenAiApi.Usage nativeUsage =\n                (org.springframework.ai.openai.api.OpenAiApi.Usage) usage.getNativeUsage();\n\n            // Detailed prompt token information\n            System.out.println(\"Prompt Tokens Details:\");\n            System.out.println(\"- Audio Tokens: \" + nativeUsage.promptTokensDetails().audioTokens());\n            System.out.println(\"- Cached Tokens: \" + nativeUsage.promptTokensDetails().cachedTokens());\n\n            // Detailed completion token information\n            System.out.println(\"Completion Tokens Details:\");\n            System.out.println(\"- Reasoning Tokens: \" + nativeUsage.completionTokenDetails().reasoningTokens());\n            System.out.println(\"- Accepted Prediction Tokens: \" + nativeUsage.completionTokenDetails().acceptedPredictionTokens());\n            System.out.println(\"- Audio Tokens: \" + nativeUsage.completionTokenDetails().audioTokens());\n            System.out.println(\"- Rejected Prediction Tokens: \" + nativeUsage.completionTokenDetails().rejectedPredictionTokens());\n        }\n    }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Using with ChatClient\n\nIf you are using the&#96;ChatClient&#96;, you can access the usage information using the&#96;ChatResponse&#96; object:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// Create a chat prompt\nPrompt prompt = new Prompt(\"What is the weather like today?\");\n\n// Create a chat client\nChatClient chatClient = ChatClient.create(chatModel);\n\n// Get the chat response\nChatResponse response = chatClient.prompt(prompt)\n        .call()\n        .chatResponse();\n\n// Access the usage information\nUsage usage = response.getMetadata().getUsage();\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Benefits\n\nStandardization: Provides a consistent way to handle usage across different AI models Flexibility: Supports model-specific usage data through the native usage feature Simplification: Reduces boilerplate code with the default implementation Extensibility: Easy to extend for specific model requirements while maintaining compatibility\n\n### Type Safety Considerations\n\nWhen working with native usage data, consider type casting carefully:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// Safe way to access native usage\nif (usage.getNativeUsage() instanceof org.springframework.ai.openai.api.OpenAiApi.Usage) {\n    org.springframework.ai.openai.api.OpenAiApi.Usage nativeUsage =\n        (org.springframework.ai.openai.api.OpenAiApi.Usage) usage.getNativeUsage();\n    // Work with native usage data\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;",
  "url": "https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/usage-handling.html"
}

{
  "markdown": "Chat Client API :: Spring AI Reference\n\n# Chat Client API\n\nThe&#96;ChatClient&#96; offers a fluent API for communicating with an AI Model. It supports both a synchronous and streaming programming model.\n\nSee the Implementation Notes at the bottom of this document related to the combined use of imperative and reactive programming models in&#96;ChatClient&#96;\n\nThe fluent API has methods for building up the constituent parts of a [Prompt](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/prompt.html#_prompt) that is passed to the AI model as input. The&#96;Prompt&#96; contains the instructional text to guide the AI model’s output and behavior. From the API point of view, prompts consist of a collection of messages.\n\nThe AI model processes two main types of messages: user messages, which are direct inputs from the user, and system messages, which are generated by the system to guide the conversation.\n\nThese messages often contain placeholders that are substituted at runtime based on user input to customize the response of the AI model to the user input.\n\nThere are also Prompt options that can be specified, such as the name of the AI Model to use and the temperature setting that controls the randomness or creativity of the generated output.\n\n## Creating a ChatClient\n\nThe&#96;ChatClient&#96; is created using a&#96;ChatClient.Builder&#96; object. You can obtain an autoconfigured&#96;ChatClient.Builder&#96; instance for any [ChatModel](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chatmodel.html) Spring Boot autoconfiguration or create one programmatically.\n\n### Using an autoconfigured ChatClient.Builder\n\nIn the most simple use case, Spring AI provides Spring Boot autoconfiguration, creating a prototype&#96;ChatClient.Builder&#96; bean for you to inject into your class. Here is a simple example of retrieving a&#96;String&#96; response to a simple user request.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@RestController\nclass MyController {\n\n    private final ChatClient chatClient;\n\n    public MyController(ChatClient.Builder chatClientBuilder) {\n        this.chatClient = chatClientBuilder.build();\n    }\n\n    @GetMapping(\"/ai\")\n    String generation(String userInput) {\n        return this.chatClient.prompt()\n            .user(userInput)\n            .call()\n            .content();\n    }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nIn this simple example, the user input sets the contents of the user message. The&#96;call()&#96; method sends a request to the AI model, and the&#96;content()&#96; method returns the AI model’s response as a&#96;String&#96;.\n\n### Working with Multiple Chat Models\n\nThere are several scenarios where you might need to work with multiple chat models in a single application:\n\nUsing different models for different types of tasks (e.g., a powerful model for complex reasoning and a faster, cheaper model for simpler tasks)\n\nImplementing fallback mechanisms when one model service is unavailable\n\nA/B testing different models or configurations\n\nProviding users with a choice of models based on their preferences\n\nCombining specialized models (one for code generation, another for creative content, etc.)\n\nBy default, Spring AI autoconfigures a single&#96;ChatClient.Builder&#96; bean. However, you may need to work with multiple chat models in your application. Here’s how to handle this scenario:\n\nIn all cases, you need to disable the&#96;ChatClient.Builder&#96; autoconfiguration by setting the property&#96;spring.ai.chat.client.enabled=false&#96;.\n\nThis allows you to create multiple&#96;ChatClient&#96; instances manually.\n\n#### Multiple ChatClients with a Single Model Type\n\nThis section covers a common use case where you need to create multiple ChatClient instances that all use the same underlying model type but with different configurations.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// Create ChatClient instances programmatically\nChatModel myChatModel = ... // already autoconfigured by Spring Boot\nChatClient chatClient = ChatClient.create(myChatModel);\n\n// Or use the builder for more control\nChatClient.Builder builder = ChatClient.builder(myChatModel);\nChatClient customChatClient = builder\n    .defaultSystemPrompt(\"You are a helpful assistant.\")\n    .build();\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n#### ChatClients for Different Model Types\n\nWhen working with multiple AI models, you can define separate&#96;ChatClient&#96; beans for each model:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nimport org.springframework.ai.chat.ChatClient;\nimport org.springframework.context.annotation.Bean;\nimport org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration;\n\n@Configuration\npublic class ChatClientConfig {\n\n    @Bean\n    public ChatClient openAiChatClient(OpenAiChatModel chatModel) {\n        return ChatClient.create(chatModel);\n    }\n\n    @Bean\n    public ChatClient anthropicChatClient(AnthropicChatModel chatModel) {\n        return ChatClient.create(chatModel);\n    }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can then inject these beans into your application components using the&#96;@Qualifier&#96; annotation:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@Configuration\npublic class ChatClientExample {\n\n    @Bean\n    CommandLineRunner cli(\n            @Qualifier(\"openAiChatClient\") ChatClient openAiChatClient,\n            @Qualifier(\"anthropicChatClient\") ChatClient anthropicChatClient) {\n\n        return args -\u003e {\n            var scanner = new Scanner(System.in);\n            ChatClient chat;\n\n            // Model selection\n            System.out.println(\"\\nSelect your AI model:\");\n            System.out.println(\"1. OpenAI\");\n            System.out.println(\"2. Anthropic\");\n            System.out.print(\"Enter your choice (1 or 2): \");\n\n            String choice = scanner.nextLine().trim();\n\n            if (choice.equals(\"1\")) {\n                chat = openAiChatClient;\n                System.out.println(\"Using OpenAI model\");\n            } else {\n                chat = anthropicChatClient;\n                System.out.println(\"Using Anthropic model\");\n            }\n\n            // Use the selected chat client\n            System.out.print(\"\\nEnter your question: \");\n            String input = scanner.nextLine();\n            String response = chat.prompt(input).call().content();\n            System.out.println(\"ASSISTANT: \" + response);\n\n            scanner.close();\n        };\n    }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n#### Multiple OpenAI-Compatible API Endpoints\n\nThe&#96;OpenAiApi&#96; and&#96;OpenAiChatModel&#96; classes provide a&#96;mutate()&#96; method that allows you to create variations of existing instances with different properties. This is particularly useful when you need to work with multiple OpenAI-compatible APIs.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@Service\npublic class MultiModelService {\n\n    private static final Logger logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MultiModelService.class);\n\n    @Autowired\n    private OpenAiChatModel baseChatModel;\n\n    @Autowired\n    private OpenAiApi baseOpenAiApi;\n\n    public void multiClientFlow() {\n        try {\n            // Derive a new OpenAiApi for Groq (Llama3)\n            OpenAiApi groqApi = baseOpenAiApi.mutate()\n                .baseUrl(\"https://api.groq.com/openai\")\n                .apiKey(System.getenv(\"GROQ_API_KEY\"))\n                .build();\n\n            // Derive a new OpenAiApi for OpenAI GPT-4\n            OpenAiApi gpt4Api = baseOpenAiApi.mutate()\n                .baseUrl(\"https://api.openai.com\")\n                .apiKey(System.getenv(\"OPENAI_API_KEY\"))\n                .build();\n\n            // Derive a new OpenAiChatModel for Groq\n            OpenAiChatModel groqModel = baseChatModel.mutate()\n                .openAiApi(groqApi)\n                .defaultOptions(OpenAiChatOptions.builder().model(\"llama3-70b-8192\").temperature(0.5).build())\n                .build();\n\n            // Derive a new OpenAiChatModel for GPT-4\n            OpenAiChatModel gpt4Model = baseChatModel.mutate()\n                .openAiApi(gpt4Api)\n                .defaultOptions(OpenAiChatOptions.builder().model(\"gpt-4\").temperature(0.7).build())\n                .build();\n\n            // Simple prompt for both models\n            String prompt = \"What is the capital of France?\";\n\n            String groqResponse = ChatClient.builder(groqModel).build().prompt(prompt).call().content();\n            String gpt4Response = ChatClient.builder(gpt4Model).build().prompt(prompt).call().content();\n\n            logger.info(\"Groq (Llama3) response: {}\", groqResponse);\n            logger.info(\"OpenAI GPT-4 response: {}\", gpt4Response);\n        }\n        catch (Exception e) {\n            logger.error(\"Error in multi-client flow\", e);\n        }\n    }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## ChatClient Fluent API\n\nThe&#96;ChatClient&#96; fluent API allows you to create a prompt in three distinct ways using an overloaded&#96;prompt&#96; method to initiate the fluent API:\n\n&#96;prompt()&#96;: This method with no arguments lets you start using the fluent API, allowing you to build up user, system, and other parts of the prompt.\n\n&#96;prompt(Prompt prompt)&#96;: This method accepts a&#96;Prompt&#96; argument, letting you pass in a&#96;Prompt&#96; instance that you have created using the Prompt’s non-fluent APIs.\n\n&#96;prompt(String content)&#96;: This is a convenience method similar to the previous overload. It takes the user’s text content.\n\n## ChatClient Responses\n\nThe&#96;ChatClient&#96; API offers several ways to format the response from the AI Model using the fluent API.\n\n### Returning a ChatResponse\n\nThe response from the AI model is a rich structure defined by the type [ChatResponse](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chatmodel.html#ChatResponse). It includes metadata about how the response was generated and can also contain multiple responses, known as [Generation](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chatmodel.html#Generation) s, each with its own metadata. The metadata includes the number of tokens (each token is approximately 3/4 of a word) used to create the response. This information is important because hosted AI models charge based on the number of tokens used per request.\n\nAn example to return the&#96;ChatResponse&#96; object that contains the metadata is shown below by invoking&#96;chatResponse()&#96; after the&#96;call()&#96; method.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nChatResponse chatResponse = chatClient.prompt()\n    .user(\"Tell me a joke\")\n    .call()\n    .chatResponse();\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Returning an Entity\n\nYou often want to return an entity class that is mapped from the returned&#96;String&#96;. The&#96;entity()&#96; method provides this functionality.\n\nFor example, given the Java record:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nrecord ActorFilms(String actor, List\u003cString\u003e movies) {}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can easily map the AI model’s output to this record using the&#96;entity()&#96; method, as shown below:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nActorFilms actorFilms = chatClient.prompt()\n    .user(\"Generate the filmography for a random actor.\")\n    .call()\n    .entity(ActorFilms.class);\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThere is also an overloaded&#96;entity&#96; method with the signature&#96;entity(ParameterizedTypeReference type)&#96; that lets you specify types such as generic Lists:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nList\u003cActorFilms\u003e actorFilms = chatClient.prompt()\n    .user(\"Generate the filmography of 5 movies for Tom Hanks and Bill Murray.\")\n    .call()\n    .entity(new ParameterizedTypeReference\u003cList\u003cActorFilms\u003e\u003e() {});\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n#### Native Structured Output\n\nAs more AI models support structured output natively, you can take advantage of this feature by using the&#96;AdvisorParams.ENABLE_NATIVE_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT&#96; advisor parameter when calling the&#96;ChatClient&#96;. You can use the&#96;defaultAdvisors()&#96; method on the&#96;ChatClient.Builder&#96; to set this parameter globally for all calls or set it per call as shown below:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nActorFilms actorFilms = chatClient.prompt()\n    .advisors(AdvisorParams.ENABLE_NATIVE_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT)\n    .user(\"Generate the filmography for a random actor.\")\n    .call()\n    .entity(ActorFilms.class);\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n| Some AI models such as OpenAI don’t support arrays of objects natively. In such cases, you can use the Spring AI default structured output conversion. |\n| --- |\n\n### Streaming Responses\n\nThe&#96;stream()&#96; method lets you get an asynchronous response as shown below:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nFlux\u003cString\u003e output = chatClient.prompt()\n    .user(\"Tell me a joke\")\n    .stream()\n    .content();\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can also stream the&#96;ChatResponse&#96; using the method&#96;Flux chatResponse()&#96;.\n\nIn the future, we will offer a convenience method that will let you return a Java entity with the reactive&#96;stream()&#96; method. In the meantime, you should use the [Structured Output Converter](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/structured-output-converter.html#StructuredOutputConverter) to convert the aggregated response explicitly as shown below. This also demonstrates the use of parameters in the fluent API that will be discussed in more detail in a later section of the documentation.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nvar converter = new BeanOutputConverter\u003c\u003e(new ParameterizedTypeReference\u003cList\u003cActorsFilms\u003e\u003e() {});\n\nFlux\u003cString\u003e flux = this.chatClient.prompt()\n    .user(u -\u003e u.text(\"\"\"\n                        Generate the filmography for a random actor.\n                        {format}\n                      \"\"\")\n            .param(\"format\", this.converter.getFormat()))\n    .stream()\n    .content();\n\nString content = this.flux.collectList().block().stream().collect(Collectors.joining());\n\nList\u003cActorsFilms\u003e actorFilms = this.converter.convert(this.content);\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Prompt Templates\n\nThe&#96;ChatClient&#96; fluent API lets you provide user and system text as templates with variables that are replaced at runtime.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nString answer = ChatClient.create(chatModel).prompt()\n    .user(u -\u003e u\n            .text(\"Tell me the names of 5 movies whose soundtrack was composed by {composer}\")\n            .param(\"composer\", \"John Williams\"))\n    .call()\n    .content();\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nInternally, the ChatClient uses the&#96;PromptTemplate&#96; class to handle the user and system text and replace the variables with the values provided at runtime relying on a given&#96;TemplateRenderer&#96; implementation. By default, Spring AI uses the&#96;StTemplateRenderer&#96; implementation, which is based on the open-source [StringTemplate](https://www.stringtemplate.org/) engine developed by Terence Parr.\n\nSpring AI also provides a&#96;NoOpTemplateRenderer&#96; for cases where no template processing is desired.\n\n| The&#96;TemplateRenderer&#96; configured directly on the&#96;ChatClient&#96;(via&#96;.templateRenderer()&#96;) applies only to the prompt content defined directly in the&#96;ChatClient&#96; builder chain (e.g., via&#96;.user()&#96;,&#96;.system()&#96;). It does not affect templates used internally by [Advisors](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/retrieval-augmented-generation.html#_questionansweradvisor) like&#96;QuestionAnswerAdvisor&#96;, which have their own template customization mechanisms (see [Custom Advisor Templates](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/retrieval-augmented-generation.html#_custom_template)). |\n| --- |\n\nIf you’d rather use a different template engine, you can provide a custom implementation of the&#96;TemplateRenderer&#96; interface directly to the ChatClient. You can also keep using the default&#96;StTemplateRenderer&#96;, but with a custom configuration.\n\nFor example, by default, template variables are identified by the&#96;{}&#96; syntax. If you’re planning to include JSON in your prompt, you might want to use a different syntax to avoid conflicts with JSON syntax. For example, you can use the&#96;\u003c&#96; and&#96;\u003e&#96; delimiters.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nString answer = ChatClient.create(chatModel).prompt()\n    .user(u -\u003e u\n            .text(\"Tell me the names of 5 movies whose soundtrack was composed by \u003ccomposer\u003e\")\n            .param(\"composer\", \"John Williams\"))\n    .templateRenderer(StTemplateRenderer.builder().startDelimiterToken('\u003c').endDelimiterToken('\u003e').build())\n    .call()\n    .content();\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## call() return values\n\nAfter specifying the&#96;call()&#96; method on&#96;ChatClient&#96;, there are a few different options for the response type.\n\n&#96;String content()&#96;: returns the String content of the response\n\n&#96;ChatResponse chatResponse()&#96;: returns the&#96;ChatResponse&#96; object that contains multiple generations and also metadata about the response, for example how many token were used to create the response.\n\n&#96;ChatClientResponse chatClientResponse()&#96;: returns a&#96;ChatClientResponse&#96; object that contains the&#96;ChatResponse&#96; object and the ChatClient execution context, giving you access to additional data used during the execution of advisors (e.g. the relevant documents retrieved in a RAG flow).\n\n&#96;entity()&#96; to return a Java type\n\n&#96;entity(ParameterizedTypeReference type)&#96;: used to return a&#96;Collection&#96; of entity types.\n\n&#96;entity(Class type)&#96;: used to return a specific entity type.\n\n&#96;entity(StructuredOutputConverter structuredOutputConverter)&#96;: used to specify an instance of a&#96;StructuredOutputConverter&#96; to convert a&#96;String&#96; to an entity type.\n\n&#96;responseEntity()&#96; to return both the&#96;ChatResponse&#96; and a Java type. This is useful when you need access to both the complete AI model response (with metadata and generations) and the structured output entity in a single call.\n\n&#96;responseEntity(Class type)&#96;: used to return a&#96;ResponseEntity&#96; containing both the complete&#96;ChatResponse&#96; object and a specific entity type.\n\n&#96;responseEntity(ParameterizedTypeReference type)&#96;: used to return a&#96;ResponseEntity&#96; containing both the complete&#96;ChatResponse&#96; object and a&#96;Collection&#96; of entity types.\n\n&#96;responseEntity(StructuredOutputConverter structuredOutputConverter)&#96;: used to return a&#96;ResponseEntity&#96; containing both the complete&#96;ChatResponse&#96; object and an entity converted using a specified&#96;StructuredOutputConverter&#96;.\n\nYou can also invoke the&#96;stream()&#96; method instead of&#96;call()&#96;.\n\n| Calling the&#96;call()&#96; method does not actually trigger the AI model execution. Instead, it only instructs Spring AI whether to use synchronous or streaming calls. The actual AI model invocation occurs when methods such as&#96;content()&#96;,&#96;chatResponse()&#96;, and&#96;responseEntity()&#96; are called. |\n| --- |\n\n## stream() return values\n\nAfter specifying the&#96;stream()&#96; method on&#96;ChatClient&#96;, there are a few options for the response type:\n\n&#96;Flux content()&#96;: Returns a&#96;Flux&#96; of the string being generated by the AI model.\n\n&#96;Flux chatResponse()&#96;: Returns a&#96;Flux&#96; of the&#96;ChatResponse&#96; object, which contains additional metadata about the response.\n\n&#96;Flux chatClientResponse()&#96;: returns a&#96;Flux&#96; of the&#96;ChatClientResponse&#96; object that contains the&#96;ChatResponse&#96; object and the ChatClient execution context, giving you access to additional data used during the execution of advisors (e.g. the relevant documents retrieved in a RAG flow).\n\n## Message Metadata\n\nThe ChatClient supports adding metadata to both user and system messages. Metadata provides additional context and information about messages that can be used by the AI model or downstream processing.\n\n### Adding Metadata to User Messages\n\nYou can add metadata to user messages using the&#96;metadata()&#96; methods:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// Adding individual metadata key-value pairs\nString response = chatClient.prompt()\n    .user(u -\u003e u.text(\"What's the weather like?\")\n        .metadata(\"messageId\", \"msg-123\")\n        .metadata(\"userId\", \"user-456\")\n        .metadata(\"priority\", \"high\"))\n    .call()\n    .content();\n\n// Adding multiple metadata entries at once\nMap\u003cString, Object\u003e userMetadata = Map.of(\n    \"messageId\", \"msg-123\",\n    \"userId\", \"user-456\",\n    \"timestamp\", System.currentTimeMillis()\n);\n\nString response = chatClient.prompt()\n    .user(u -\u003e u.text(\"What's the weather like?\")\n        .metadata(userMetadata))\n    .call()\n    .content();\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Adding Metadata to System Messages\n\nSimilarly, you can add metadata to system messages:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// Adding metadata to system messages\nString response = chatClient.prompt()\n    .system(s -\u003e s.text(\"You are a helpful assistant.\")\n        .metadata(\"version\", \"1.0\")\n        .metadata(\"model\", \"gpt-4\"))\n    .user(\"Tell me a joke\")\n    .call()\n    .content();\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Default Metadata Support\n\nYou can also configure default metadata at the ChatClient builder level:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@Configuration\nclass Config {\n    @Bean\n    ChatClient chatClient(ChatClient.Builder builder) {\n        return builder\n            .defaultSystem(s -\u003e s.text(\"You are a helpful assistant\")\n                .metadata(\"assistantType\", \"general\")\n                .metadata(\"version\", \"1.0\"))\n            .defaultUser(u -\u003e u.text(\"Default user context\")\n                .metadata(\"sessionId\", \"default-session\"))\n            .build();\n    }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Metadata Validation\n\nThe ChatClient validates metadata to ensure data integrity:\n\nMetadata keys cannot be null or empty\n\nMetadata values cannot be null\n\nWhen passing a Map, neither keys nor values can contain null elements\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// This will throw an IllegalArgumentException\nchatClient.prompt()\n    .user(u -\u003e u.text(\"Hello\")\n        .metadata(null, \"value\"))  // Invalid: null key\n    .call()\n    .content();\n\n// This will also throw an IllegalArgumentException\nchatClient.prompt()\n    .user(u -\u003e u.text(\"Hello\")\n        .metadata(\"key\", null))    // Invalid: null value\n    .call()\n    .content();\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Accessing Metadata\n\nThe metadata is included in the generated UserMessage and SystemMessage objects and can be accessed through the message’s&#96;getMetadata()&#96; method. This is particularly useful when processing messages in advisors or when examining the conversation history.\n\n## Using Defaults\n\nCreating a&#96;ChatClient&#96; with a default system text in an&#96;@Configuration&#96; class simplifies runtime code. By setting defaults, you only need to specify the user text when calling&#96;ChatClient&#96;, eliminating the need to set a system text for each request in your runtime code path.\n\n### Default System Text\n\nIn the following example, we will configure the system text to always reply in a pirate’s voice. To avoid repeating the system text in runtime code, we will create a&#96;ChatClient&#96; instance in a&#96;@Configuration&#96; class.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@Configuration\nclass Config {\n\n    @Bean\n    ChatClient chatClient(ChatClient.Builder builder) {\n        return builder.defaultSystem(\"You are a friendly chat bot that answers question in the voice of a Pirate\")\n                .build();\n    }\n\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nand a&#96;@RestController&#96; to invoke it:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@RestController\nclass AIController {\n\n\tprivate final ChatClient chatClient;\n\n\tAIController(ChatClient chatClient) {\n\t\tthis.chatClient = chatClient;\n\t}\n\n\t@GetMapping(\"/ai/simple\")\n\tpublic Map\u003cString, String\u003e completion(@RequestParam(value = \"message\", defaultValue = \"Tell me a joke\") String message) {\n\t\treturn Map.of(\"completion\", this.chatClient.prompt().user(message).call().content());\n\t}\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nWhen calling the application endpoint via curl, the result is:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n❯ curl localhost:8080/ai/simple\n{\"completion\":\"Why did the pirate go to the comedy club? To hear some arrr-rated jokes! Arrr, matey!\"}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Default System Text with parameters\n\nIn the following example, we will use a placeholder in the system text to specify the voice of the completion at runtime instead of design time.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@Configuration\nclass Config {\n\n    @Bean\n    ChatClient chatClient(ChatClient.Builder builder) {\n        return builder.defaultSystem(\"You are a friendly chat bot that answers question in the voice of a {voice}\")\n                .build();\n    }\n\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@RestController\nclass AIController {\n\tprivate final ChatClient chatClient;\n\n\tAIController(ChatClient chatClient) {\n\t\tthis.chatClient = chatClient;\n\t}\n\n\t@GetMapping(\"/ai\")\n\tMap\u003cString, String\u003e completion(@RequestParam(value = \"message\", defaultValue = \"Tell me a joke\") String message, String voice) {\n\t\treturn Map.of(\"completion\",\n\t\t\t\tthis.chatClient.prompt()\n\t\t\t\t\t\t.system(sp -\u003e sp.param(\"voice\", voice))\n\t\t\t\t\t\t.user(message)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t.call()\n\t\t\t\t\t\t.content());\n\t}\n\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nWhen calling the application endpoint via httpie, the result is:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nhttp localhost:8080/ai voice=='Robert DeNiro'\n{\n    \"completion\": \"You talkin' to me? Okay, here's a joke for ya: Why couldn't the bicycle stand up by itself? Because it was two tired! Classic, right?\"\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Other defaults\n\nAt the&#96;ChatClient.Builder&#96; level, you can specify the default prompt configuration.\n\n&#96;defaultOptions(ChatOptions chatOptions)&#96;: Pass in either portable options defined in the&#96;ChatOptions&#96; class or model-specific options such as those in&#96;OpenAiChatOptions&#96;. For more information on model-specific&#96;ChatOptions&#96; implementations, refer to the JavaDocs.\n\n&#96;defaultFunction(String name, String description, java.util.function.Function function)&#96;: The&#96;name&#96; is used to refer to the function in user text. The&#96;description&#96; explains the function’s purpose and helps the AI model choose the correct function for an accurate response. The&#96;function&#96; argument is a Java function instance that the model will execute when necessary.\n\n&#96;defaultFunctions(String…​ functionNames)&#96;: The bean names of &#96;java.util.Function&#96;s defined in the application context.\n\n&#96;defaultUser(String text)&#96;,&#96;defaultUser(Resource text)&#96;,&#96;defaultUser(Consumer userSpecConsumer)&#96;: These methods let you define the user text. The&#96;Consumer &#96; allows you to use a lambda to specify the user text and any default parameters.\n\n&#96;defaultAdvisors(Advisor…​ advisor)&#96;: Advisors allow modification of the data used to create the&#96;Prompt&#96;. The&#96;QuestionAnswerAdvisor&#96; implementation enables the pattern of&#96;Retrieval Augmented Generation&#96; by appending the prompt with context information related to the user text.\n\n&#96;defaultAdvisors(Consumer advisorSpecConsumer)&#96;: This method allows you to define a&#96;Consumer&#96; to configure multiple advisors using the&#96;AdvisorSpec&#96;. Advisors can modify the data used to create the final&#96;Prompt&#96;. The&#96;Consumer &#96; lets you specify a lambda to add advisors, such as&#96;QuestionAnswerAdvisor&#96;, which supports&#96;Retrieval Augmented Generation&#96; by appending the prompt with relevant context information based on the user text.\n\nYou can override these defaults at runtime using the corresponding methods without the&#96;default&#96; prefix.\n\n&#96;options(ChatOptions chatOptions)&#96;\n\n&#96;function(String name, String description,\njava.util.function.Function function)&#96;\n\n&#96;functions(String…​ functionNames)&#96;\n\n&#96;user(String text)&#96;,&#96;user(Resource text)&#96;,&#96;user(Consumer userSpecConsumer)&#96;\n\n&#96;advisors(Advisor…​ advisor)&#96;\n\n&#96;advisors(Consumer advisorSpecConsumer)&#96;\n\n## Advisors\n\nThe [Advisors API](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/advisors.html) provides a flexible and powerful way to intercept, modify, and enhance AI-driven interactions in your Spring applications.\n\nA common pattern when calling an AI model with user text is to append or augment the prompt with contextual data.\n\nThis contextual data can be of different types. Common types include:\n\nYour own data: This is data the AI model hasn’t been trained on. Even if the model has seen similar data, the appended contextual data takes precedence in generating the response.\n\nConversational history: The chat model’s API is stateless. If you tell the AI model your name, it won’t remember it in subsequent interactions. Conversational history must be sent with each request to ensure previous interactions are considered when generating a response.\n\n### Advisor Configuration in ChatClient\n\nThe ChatClient fluent API provides an&#96;AdvisorSpec&#96; interface for configuring advisors. This interface offers methods to add parameters, set multiple parameters at once, and add one or more advisors to the chain.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\ninterface AdvisorSpec {\n    AdvisorSpec param(String k, Object v);\n    AdvisorSpec params(Map\u003cString, Object\u003e p);\n    AdvisorSpec advisors(Advisor... advisors);\n    AdvisorSpec advisors(List\u003cAdvisor\u003e advisors);\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n| The order in which advisors are added to the chain is crucial, as it determines the sequence of their execution. Each advisor modifies the prompt or the context in some way, and the changes made by one advisor are passed on to the next in the chain. |\n| --- |\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nChatClient.builder(chatModel)\n    .build()\n    .prompt()\n    .advisors(\n        MessageChatMemoryAdvisor.builder(chatMemory).build(),\n        QuestionAnswerAdvisor.builder(vectorStore).build()\n    )\n    .user(userText)\n    .call()\n    .content();\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nIn this configuration, the&#96;MessageChatMemoryAdvisor&#96; will be executed first, adding the conversation history to the prompt. Then, the&#96;QuestionAnswerAdvisor&#96; will perform its search based on the user’s question and the added conversation history, potentially providing more relevant results.\n\n[Learn about Question Answer Advisor](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/retrieval-augmented-generation.html#_questionansweradvisor)\n\n### Retrieval Augmented Generation\n\nRefer to the [Retrieval Augmented Generation](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/retrieval-augmented-generation.html) guide.\n\n### Logging\n\nThe&#96;SimpleLoggerAdvisor&#96; is an advisor that logs the&#96;request&#96; and&#96;response&#96; data of the&#96;ChatClient&#96;. This can be useful for debugging and monitoring your AI interactions.\n\n| Spring AI supports observability for LLM and vector store interactions. Refer to the [Observability](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/observability/index.html) guide for more information. |\n| --- |\n\nTo enable logging, add the&#96;SimpleLoggerAdvisor&#96; to the advisor chain when creating your ChatClient. It’s recommended to add it toward the end of the chain:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nChatResponse response = ChatClient.create(chatModel).prompt()\n        .advisors(new SimpleLoggerAdvisor())\n        .user(\"Tell me a joke?\")\n        .call()\n        .chatResponse();\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nTo see the logs, set the logging level for the advisor package to&#96;DEBUG&#96;:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nlogging.level.org.springframework.ai.chat.client.advisor=DEBUG\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nAdd this to your&#96;application.properties&#96; or&#96;application.yaml&#96; file.\n\nYou can customize what data from&#96;AdvisedRequest&#96; and&#96;ChatResponse&#96; is logged by using the following constructor:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nSimpleLoggerAdvisor(\n    Function\u003cChatClientRequest, String\u003e requestToString,\n    Function\u003cChatResponse, String\u003e responseToString,\n    int order\n)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nExample usage:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nSimpleLoggerAdvisor customLogger = new SimpleLoggerAdvisor(\n    request -\u003e \"Custom request: \" + request.prompt().getUserMessage(),\n    response -\u003e \"Custom response: \" + response.getResult(),\n    0\n);\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThis allows you to tailor the logged information to your specific needs.\n\n| Be cautious about logging sensitive information in production environments. |\n| --- |\n\n## Chat Memory\n\nThe interface&#96;ChatMemory&#96; represents a storage for chat conversation memory. It provides methods to add messages to a conversation, retrieve messages from a conversation, and clear the conversation history.\n\nThere is currently one built-in implementation:&#96;MessageWindowChatMemory&#96;.\n\n&#96;MessageWindowChatMemory&#96; is a chat memory implementation that maintains a window of messages up to a specified maximum size (default: 20 messages). When the number of messages exceeds this limit, older messages are evicted, but system messages are preserved. If a new system message is added, all previous system messages are removed from memory. This ensures that the most recent context is always available for the conversation while keeping memory usage bounded.\n\nThe&#96;MessageWindowChatMemory&#96; is backed by the&#96;ChatMemoryRepository&#96; abstraction which provides storage implementations for the chat conversation memory. There are several implementations available, including the&#96;InMemoryChatMemoryRepository&#96;,&#96;JdbcChatMemoryRepository&#96;,&#96;CassandraChatMemoryRepository&#96; and&#96;Neo4jChatMemoryRepository&#96;.\n\nFor more details and usage examples, see the [Chat Memory](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat-memory.html) documentation.\n\n## Implementation Notes\n\nThe combined use of imperative and reactive programming models in&#96;ChatClient&#96; is a unique aspect of the API. Often an application will be either reactive or imperative, but not both.\n\nWhen customizing the HTTP client interactions of a Model implementation, both the RestClient and the WebClient must be configured.\n\nDue to a bug in Spring Boot 3.4, the \"spring.http.client.factory=jdk\" property must be set. Otherwise, it’s set to \"reactor\" by default, which breaks certain AI workflows like the ImageModel.\n\nStreaming is only supported via the Reactive stack. Imperative applications must include the Reactive stack for this reason (e.g. spring-boot-starter-webflux).\n\nNon-streaming is only supportive via the Servlet stack. Reactive applications must include the Servlet stack for this reason (e.g. spring-boot-starter-web) and expect some calls to be blocking.\n\nTool calling is imperative, leading to blocking workflows. This also results in partial/interrupted Micrometer observations (e.g. the ChatClient spans and the tool calling spans are not connected, with the first one remaining incomplete for that reason).\n\nThe built-in advisors perform blocking operations for standards calls, and non-blocking operations for streaming calls. The Reactor Scheduler used for the advisor streaming calls can be configured via the Builder on each Advisor class.",
  "url": "https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chatclient.html"
}

{
  "markdown": "Observability :: Spring AI Reference\n\n# Observability\n\nSpring AI builds upon the observability features in the Spring ecosystem to provide insights into AI-related operations.\n\nThe spring-boot-actuator module is required for enabling observability. Add the Spring Boot Actuator dependency to your project’s Maven&#96;pom.xml&#96; build file:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003cdependency\u003e\n \u003cgroupId\u003eorg.springframework.boot\u003c/groupId\u003e\n \u003cartifactId\u003espring-boot-starter-actuator\u003c/artifactId\u003e\n\u003c/dependency\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nor to your Gradle&#96;build.gradle&#96; build file.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\ndependencies {\n    implementation 'org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-actuator'\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nSpring AI provides metrics and tracing capabilities for its core components:&#96;ChatClient&#96;(including&#96;Advisor&#96;),&#96;ChatModel&#96;,&#96;EmbeddingModel&#96;,&#96;ImageModel&#96;, and&#96;VectorStore&#96;.\n\n| Low cardinality keys will be added to metrics and traces, while high cardinality keys will only be added to traces. |\n| --- |\n\n1.0.0-RC1 Breaking Changes\n\nFollowing configuration properties have been renamed to better reflect their purpose:\n\n&#96;spring.ai.chat.client.observations.include-prompt&#96;→&#96;spring.ai.chat.client.observations.log-prompt&#96;\n\n&#96;spring.ai.chat.observations.include-prompt&#96;→&#96;spring.ai.chat.observations.log-prompt&#96;\n\n&#96;spring.ai.chat.observations.include-completion&#96;→&#96;spring.ai.chat.observations.log-completion&#96;\n\n&#96;spring.ai.image.observations.include-prompt&#96;→&#96;spring.ai.image.observations.log-prompt&#96;\n\n&#96;spring.ai.vectorstore.observations.include-query-response&#96;→&#96;spring.ai.vectorstore.observations.log-query-response&#96;\n\n## Chat Client\n\nThe&#96;spring.ai.chat.client&#96; observations are recorded when a ChatClient&#96;call()&#96; or&#96;stream()&#96; operations are invoked. They measure the time spent performing the invocation and propagate the related tracing information.\n\n&#96;gen_ai.operation.name&#96;\n\nAlways&#96;framework&#96;.\n\n&#96;gen_ai.system&#96;\n\nAlways&#96;spring_ai&#96;.\n\n&#96;spring.ai.chat.client.stream&#96;\n\nIs the chat model response a stream -&#96;true or false&#96;\n\n&#96;spring.ai.kind&#96;\n\nThe kind of framework API in Spring AI:&#96;chat_client&#96;.\n\n| Table 1. Low Cardinality Keys | Name | Description |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n\n&#96;gen_ai.prompt&#96;\n\nThe content of the prompt sent via the chat client. Optional.\n\n&#96;spring.ai.chat.client.advisor.params&#96;(deprecated)\n\nMap of advisor parameters. The conversation ID is now included in&#96;spring.ai.chat.client.conversation.id&#96;.\n\n&#96;spring.ai.chat.client.advisors&#96;\n\nList of configured chat client advisors.\n\n&#96;spring.ai.chat.client.conversation.id&#96;\n\nIdentifier of the conversation when using the chat memory.\n\n&#96;spring.ai.chat.client.system.params&#96;(deprecated)\n\nChat client system parameters. Optional. Superseded by&#96;gen_ai.prompt&#96;.\n\n&#96;spring.ai.chat.client.system.text&#96;(deprecated)\n\nChat client system text. Optional. Superseded by&#96;gen_ai.prompt&#96;.\n\n&#96;spring.ai.chat.client.tool.function.names&#96;(deprecated)\n\nEnabled tool function names. Superseded by&#96;spring.ai.chat.client.tool.names&#96;.\n\n&#96;spring.ai.chat.client.tool.function.callbacks&#96;(deprecated)\n\nList of configured chat client function callbacks. Superseded by&#96;spring.ai.chat.client.tool.names&#96;.\n\n&#96;spring.ai.chat.client.tool.names&#96;\n\nNames of the tools passed to the chat client.\n\n&#96;spring.ai.chat.client.user.params&#96;(deprecated)\n\nChat client user parameters. Please, be careful! |\n| --- |\n\n## Vector Stores\n\nAll vector store implementations in Spring AI are instrumented to provide metrics and distributed tracing data through Micrometer.\n\nThe&#96;db.vector.client.operation&#96; observations are recorded when interacting with the Vector Store. They measure the time spent on the&#96;query&#96;,&#96;add&#96; and&#96;remove&#96; operations and propagate the related tracing information.\n\n&#96;db.operation.name&#96;\n\nThe name of the operation or command being executed. One of&#96;add&#96;,&#96;delete&#96;, or&#96;query&#96;.\n\n&#96;db.system&#96;\n\nThe database management system (DBMS) product as identified by the client instrumentation. One of&#96;pg_vector&#96;,&#96;azure&#96;,&#96;cassandra&#96;,&#96;chroma&#96;,&#96;elasticsearch&#96;,&#96;milvus&#96;,&#96;neo4j&#96;,&#96;opensearch&#96;,&#96;qdrant&#96;,&#96;redis&#96;,&#96;typesense&#96;,&#96;weaviate&#96;,&#96;pinecone&#96;,&#96;oracle&#96;,&#96;mongodb&#96;,&#96;gemfire&#96;,&#96;hana&#96;,&#96;simple&#96;.\n\n&#96;spring.ai.kind&#96;\n\nThe kind of framework API in Spring AI:&#96;vector_store&#96;.\n\n| Table 13. Low Cardinality Keys | Name | Description |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n\n&#96;db.collection.name&#96;\n\nThe name of a collection (table, container) within the database.\n\n&#96;db.namespace&#96;\n\nThe name of the database, fully qualified within the server address and port.\n\n&#96;db.record.id&#96;\n\nThe record identifier if present.\n\n&#96;db.search.similarity_metric&#96;\n\nThe metric used in similarity search.\n\n&#96;db.vector.dimension_count&#96;\n\nThe dimension of the vector.\n\n&#96;db.vector.field_name&#96;\n\nThe name field as of the vector (e.g. a field name).\n\n&#96;db.vector.query.content&#96;\n\nThe content of the search query being executed.\n\n&#96;db.vector.query.filter&#96;\n\nThe metadata filters used in the search query.\n\n&#96;db.vector.query.response.documents&#96;\n\nReturned documents from a similarity search query. Optional.\n\n&#96;db.vector.query.similarity_threshold&#96;\n\nSimilarity threshold that accepts all search scores. A threshold value of 0.0 means any similarity is accepted or disable the similarity threshold filtering. A threshold value of 1.0 means an exact match is required.\n\n&#96;db.vector.query.top_k&#96;\n\nThe top-k most similar vectors returned by a query.\n\n| Table 14. High Cardinality Keys | Name | Description |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n\n### Response Data\n\nThe vector search response data is typically big and possibly containing sensitive information. For those reasons, it is not exported by default.\n\nSpring AI supports logging vector search response data, useful for troubleshooting scenarios. Please, be careful! |\n| --- |\n\n## More Metrics Reference\n\nThis section documents the metrics emitted by Spring AI components as they appear in Prometheus.\n\n### Metric Naming Conventions\n\nSpring AI uses Micrometer. Base metric names use dots (e.g.,&#96;gen_ai.client.operation&#96;), which Prometheus exports with underscores and standard suffixes:\n\nTimers →&#96; _seconds_count&#96;,&#96; _seconds_sum&#96;,&#96; _seconds_max&#96;, and (when supported)&#96; _active_count&#96;\n\nCounters →&#96; _total&#96;(monotonic)\n\nThe following shows how base metric names expand to Prometheus time series.\n\n&#96;gen_ai.client.operation&#96;\n\n&#96;gen_ai_client_operation_seconds_count&#96;&#96;gen_ai_client_operation_seconds_sum&#96;&#96;gen_ai_client_operation_seconds_max&#96;&#96;gen_ai_client_operation_active_count&#96;\n\n&#96;db.vector.client.operation&#96;\n\n&#96;db_vector_client_operation_seconds_count&#96;&#96;db_vector_client_operation_seconds_sum&#96;&#96;db_vector_client_operation_seconds_max&#96;&#96;db_vector_client_operation_active_count&#96;\n\n| Base metric name | Exported time series |\n| --- | --- |\n\n#### References\n\nOpenTelemetry — [Semantic Conventions for Generative AI (overview)](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/)\n\nMicrometer — [Naming Meters](https://docs.micrometer.io/micrometer/reference/concepts/naming.html)\n\n### Chat Client Metrics\n\n&#96;gen_ai_chat_client_operation_seconds_sum&#96;\n\nTimer\n\nseconds\n\nTotal time spent in ChatClient operations (call/stream)\n\n&#96;gen_ai_chat_client_operation_seconds_count&#96;\n\nCounter\n\ncount\n\nNumber of completed ChatClient operations\n\n&#96;gen_ai_chat_client_operation_seconds_max&#96;\n\nGauge\n\nseconds\n\nMaximum observed duration of ChatClient operations\n\n&#96;gen_ai_chat_client_operation_active_count&#96;\n\nGauge\n\ncount\n\nNumber of ChatClient operations currently in flight\n\n| Metric Name | Type | Unit | Description |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n\nActive vs Completed:&#96;active_count&#96; shows in-flight calls; the&#96;_seconds&#96; series reflect only completed calls.\n\n### Chat Model Metrics (Model provider execution)\n\n&#96;gen_ai_client_operation_seconds_sum&#96;\n\nTimer\n\nseconds\n\nTotal time executing chat model operations\n\n&#96;gen_ai_client_operation_seconds_count&#96;\n\nCounter\n\ncount\n\nNumber of completed chat model operations\n\n&#96;gen_ai_client_operation_seconds_max&#96;\n\nGauge\n\nseconds\n\nMaximum observed duration for chat model operations\n\n&#96;gen_ai_client_operation_active_count&#96;\n\nGauge\n\ncount\n\nNumber of chat model operations currently in flight\n\n| Metric Name | Type | Unit | Description |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n\n#### Token Usage\n\n&#96;gen_ai_client_token_usage_total&#96;\n\nCounter\n\ntokens\n\nTotal tokens consumed, labeled by token type\n\n| Metric Name | Type | Unit | Description |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n\n#### Labels\n\n&#96;gen_ai_token_type=input&#96;\n\nPrompt tokens sent to the model\n\n&#96;gen_ai_token_type=output&#96;\n\nCompletion tokens returned by the model\n\n&#96;gen_ai_token_type=total&#96;\n\nInput + output\n\n| Label | Meaning |\n| --- | --- |\n\n### Vector Store Metrics\n\n&#96;db_vector_client_operation_seconds_sum&#96;\n\nTimer\n\nseconds\n\nTotal time spent in vector store operations (add/delete/query)\n\n&#96;db_vector_client_operation_seconds_count&#96;\n\nCounter\n\ncount\n\nNumber of completed vector store operations\n\n&#96;db_vector_client_operation_seconds_max&#96;\n\nGauge\n\nseconds\n\nMaximum observed duration for vector store operations\n\n&#96;db_vector_client_operation_active_count&#96;\n\nGauge\n\ncount\n\nNumber of vector store operations currently in flight\n\n| Metric Name | Type | Unit | Description |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n\n#### Labels\n\n&#96;db_operation_name&#96;\n\nOperation type (&#96;add&#96;,&#96;delete&#96;,&#96;query&#96;)\n\n&#96;db_system&#96;\n\nVector DB/provider (&#96;redis&#96;,&#96;chroma&#96;,&#96;pgvector&#96;, …)\n\n&#96;spring_ai_kind&#96;\n\n&#96;vector_store&#96;\n\n| Label | Meaning |\n| --- | --- |\n\n### Understanding Active vs Completed\n\nActive (&#96;*_active_count&#96;) — instantaneous gauge of in-progress operations (concurrency/load).\n\nCompleted (&#96;*_seconds_sum|count|max&#96;) — statistics for operations that have finished:\n\n&#96;_seconds_sum / _seconds_count&#96;→ average latency\n\n&#96;_seconds_max&#96;→ high-water mark since last scrape (subject to registry behavior)",

_**Agent (gpt-5.4)**_

结合你现在这个项目，**最合适的做法是两层同时做**：

1. **业务侧落库/业务日志**：在你自己的 `AiGatewayService` 里统一记录每次调用的大模型输入、输出、耗时、token。
2. **框架侧指标监控**：打开 Spring AI Observability，把 token 指标暴露给 Actuator/Prometheus。

这样既能查“某次请求到底发了什么、回了什么”，也能看整体 token 消耗趋势。

你当前项目里，所有大模型调用几乎都收口在 `AiGatewayService`，所以这里就是最佳切入点：

```29:56:src/main/java/com/my/ai/cursor/infrastructure/ai/AiGatewayService.java
public String chat(AiScene scene, String userMessage) {
    return chatClientRouter.route(scene)
        .prompt()
        .user(userMessage)
        .call()
        .content();
}

public String chat(AiScene scene, Prompt prompt) {
    return chatClientRouter.route(scene)
        .prompt(prompt)
        .call()
        .content();
}

public <T> T chat(AiScene scene, Prompt prompt, Class<T> _class) {
    return chatClientRouter.route(scene)
        .prompt(prompt)
        .call()
        .entity(_class);
}

public Flux<String> streamChat(AiScene scene, String userMessage) {
    return chatClientRouter.route(scene)
        .prompt()
        .user(userMessage)
        .stream()
        .content();
}
```

这里目前的问题很明确：你现在直接用了 `.content()` / `.entity()`，**把 `ChatResponse` 元数据丢掉了**，所以拿不到 usage。

## 推荐方案

### 一、在 `AiGatewayService` 统一封装调用结果

Spring AI 2.x 里，token 用量要从 `ChatResponse` 取：

- `response.getMetadata().getUsage().getPromptTokens()`
- `response.getMetadata().getUsage().getCompletionTokens()`
- `response.getMetadata().getUsage().getTotalTokens()`

所以同步调用不要再直接 `.content()`，而是先拿 `chatResponse()` 或 `responseEntity()`。

你可以定义一个统一结果对象，例如：

```java
public record AiCallResult<T>(
    T data,
    String rawContent,
    Integer promptTokens,
    Integer completionTokens,
    Integer totalTokens,
    String model,
    Long elapsedMs
) {
}
```

然后把网关改成这种思路：

```java
public AiCallResult<String> chatWithLog(AiScene scene, String userMessage) {
    long start = System.currentTimeMillis();

    ChatResponse response = chatClientRouter.route(scene)
        .prompt()
        .user(userMessage)
        .call()
        .chatResponse();

    String content = response.getResult() == null ? null : response.getResult().getOutput().getText();
    Usage usage = response.getMetadata() == null ? null : response.getMetadata().getUsage();

    Integer promptTokens = usage == null ? null : usage.getPromptTokens();
    Integer completionTokens = usage == null ? null : usage.getCompletionTokens();
    Integer totalTokens = usage == null ? null : usage.getTotalTokens();

    long elapsed = System.currentTimeMillis() - start;

    // 这里写日志或落库
    log.info("LLM call scene={}, promptTokens={}, completionTokens={}, totalTokens={}, elapsedMs={}",
        scene, promptTokens, completionTokens, totalTokens, elapsed);

    return new AiCallResult<>(
        content,
        content,
        promptTokens,
        completionTokens,
        totalTokens,
        null,
        elapsed
    );
}
```

如果是结构化输出，你可以用 `responseEntity(Class<T>)`，这样既能拿到实体，也能拿到 `ChatResponse`：

```java
var responseEntity = chatClientRouter.route(scene)
    .prompt(prompt)
    .call()
    .responseEntity(ChatCleanDto.class);

ChatCleanDto dto = responseEntity.entity();
ChatResponse response = responseEntity.chatResponse();
Usage usage = response.getMetadata().getUsage();
```

这比你现在这段更适合做日志统计：

```40:44:src/main/java/com/my/ai/cursor/application/service/cleaning/TextCorrectionService.java
return aiGatewayService.chat(
    AiScene.RAG_CLEANING,
    promptTemplate.create(Map.of("message", message, "domainName", domainName)),
    ChatCleanDto.class
);
```

### 二、把“日志记录”做成独立组件

建议不要把日志拼装逻辑散在业务代码里，而是单独抽一个记录服务，比如 `LlmInvocationLogService`。

建议记录字段至少有：

- `requestId`
- `scene`
- `model`
- `systemPrompt`
- `userPrompt`
- `finalPrompt`
- `responseContent`
- `promptTokens`
- `completionTokens`
- `totalTokens`
- `elapsedMs`
- `success`
- `errorMessage`
- `createdAt`

如果你要入库，建议建一张表，比如 `llm_call_log`。

一个简单的表结构思路：

```sql
create table llm_call_log (
    id bigint primary key auto_increment,
    request_id varchar(64) not null,
    scene varchar(64) not null,
    model varchar(128),
    system_prompt longtext,
    user_prompt longtext,
    final_prompt longtext,
    response_content longtext,
    prompt_tokens int,
    completion_tokens int,
    total_tokens int,
    elapsed_ms bigint,
    success tinyint not null,
    error_message text,
    created_at datetime not null default current_timestamp
);
```

如果担心日志量太大，建议：

- `final_prompt` 和 `response_content` 做长度截断
- 或只保存摘要
- 或加开关只记录指定场景
- OCR / RAG 文本建议脱敏

## 为什么建议在 `AiGatewayService` 做，而不是只靠 Advisor

你现在的 `ChatClient` 是在配置类里手工创建的：

```72:89:src/main/java/com/my/ai/cursor/infrastructure/ai/config/ApiServiceConfig.java
@Bean("normalChatClient")
public ChatClient normalChatClient(OpenAiChatModel openAiChatModel) {
    return ChatClient.builder(openAiChatModel)
        .defaultSystem("你是一个友好的 AI 助手，请用中文准确回答用户的问题")
        .build();
}

@Bean("ragCleaningChatClient")
public ChatClient ragCleaningChatClient(@Qualifier("ragCleaningChatModel") OpenAiChatModel ragCleaningChatModel) {
    return ChatClient.builder(ragCleaningChatModel)
        .defaultSystem("""
          你是一位专业字符识别与语言规范专家，专注于识别文本中的特殊字符、易混淆字符及专业领域错误用字。
          ...
          """).build();
}
```

`Advisor` 当然可以加，Spring AI 也有 `SimpleLoggerAdvisor`，但它更适合：

- 开发期调试
- 打印请求/响应
- 做链路增强

如果你的目标是“**每次调用都要稳定落业务日志，还要带 scene、requestId、耗时、异常、token**”，那么**统一收口在 `AiGatewayService` 更稳、更贴合你现在架构**。

最实用的组合是：

- `AiGatewayService`：负责业务日志落库
- `SimpleLoggerAdvisor`：开发阶段辅助看 prompt/response
- Observability：负责整体指标

## 三、流式接口要单独处理

你现在聊天流式接口走的是：

```31:40:src/main/java/com/my/ai/cursor/interfaces/ChatController.java
@GetMapping(value = "/stream", produces = "text/event-stream;charset=UTF-8")
public Flux<String> streamChat(@RequestParam String message) {
    return chatService.streamChat(message);
}

@GetMapping(value = "/stream/rag", produces = "text/event-stream;charset=UTF-8")
public Flux<String> streamChatWithRag(@RequestParam String message,
                                      @RequestParam(defaultValue = "5") Integer topK) {
    String context = knowledgeSearchService.buildContext(message, topK);
    return chatService.streamChatWithContext(message, context);
}
```

而 `ChatService` 最终还是返回 `Flux<String>`：

```21:38:src/main/java/com/my/ai/cursor/application/service/chat/ChatService.java
public Flux<String> streamChat(String message) {
    return aiGatewayService.streamChat(AiScene.NORMAL_CHAT, message);
}

public Flux<String> streamChatWithContext(String message, String context) {
    if (context == null || context.isBlank()) {
        return streamChat(message);
    }
    String ragPrompt = """
        你是一名医学知识问答助手，请优先基于给定资料回答，若资料不足请明确说明。

       【用户问题】
         %s
       【参考资料】
         %s
       """.formatted(message, context);

    return aiGatewayService.streamChat(AiScene.NORMAL_CHAT, ragPrompt);
}
```

如果你想记录流式 token，建议把：

- `.stream().content()`

改成：

- `.stream().chatResponse()`

然后：

- 一边把 chunk 内容继续推给前端
- 一边在服务端累计 `StringBuilder`
- 在最后一个 chunk 上读取 `usage`

同时加配置：

```yaml
spring:
  ai:
    openai:
      chat:
        options:
          stream-usage: true
```

注意两点：

1. 不是所有兼容 OpenAI 的供应商都完整支持流式 usage，DashScope 兼容接口需要你实际验证。
2. 就算开启了，通常也是**最后一个 chunk**才带 usage。

所以流式调用建议封装成这种思路：

```java
Flux<ChatResponse> responseFlux = chatClientRouter.route(scene)
    .prompt()
    .user(userMessage)
    .stream()
    .chatResponse();
```

然后你自己：

- 拼接输出文本
- 在 `doOnComplete` 或最终聚合逻辑里记录 token / 耗时 / 全量输出

## 四、如果你只想“先快速看到日志”，先上 `SimpleLoggerAdvisor`

这是最快能看到输入输出的方法。

在 `ChatClient.builder(...)` 上挂：

```java
ChatClient.builder(openAiChatModel)
    .defaultAdvisors(new SimpleLoggerAdvisor())
    .defaultSystem("...")
    .build();
```

然后配置日志级别：

```yaml
logging:
  level:
    org.springframework.ai.chat.client.advisor: DEBUG
```

但这个方案我不建议作为最终方案，因为：

- 不方便统一落库
- 不方便加你自己的 `scene/requestId/userId/businessId`
- 生产环境直接打印 prompt/response 有泄露风险

## 五、再加上 Spring AI Observability 做总量统计

如果你还想看“今天总共用了多少 token、哪个模型消耗最多”，可以再加：

```xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-actuator</artifactId>
</dependency>
```

然后暴露指标端点，比如：

```yaml
management:
  endpoints:
    web:
      exposure:
        include: health,info,metrics,prometheus
```

Spring AI 会提供 token 指标：

- `gen_ai.client.token.usage`

Prometheus 下通常会看到导出后的形式：

- `gen_ai_client_token_usage_total{gen_ai_token_type="input"}`
- `gen_ai_client_token_usage_total{gen_ai_token_type="output"}`
- `gen_ai_client_token_usage_total{gen_ai_token_type="total"}`

如果只是调试，也可以临时打开：

```yaml
spring:
  ai:
    chat:
      observations:
        log-prompt: true
        log-completion: true
```

但这更偏“观测日志”，不是业务审计日志。

## 六、最贴合你当前项目的落地建议

按优先级我建议你这样做：

1. **先改 `AiGatewayService`**
   把同步调用从 `.content()` / `.entity()` 改成 `chatResponse()` / `responseEntity()`，先把 usage 拿到。

2. **新增一个日志记录服务**
   在 `AiGatewayService` 中统一调用，记录：
   - 场景 `AiScene`
   - 输入 prompt
   - 输出内容
   - token
   - 耗时
   - 成功/失败

3. **流式接口单独改造**
   把 `Flux<String>` 的来源改成 `Flux<ChatResponse>`，边推流边累计，最后记 usage。

4. **接入 Actuator**
   用于整体 token 监控和告警。

5. **加脱敏和开关**
   因为你这里既有普通问答，也有 OCR/RAG 文本，日志很容易包含敏感数据。

## 一个现实提醒

你项目里现在配置里直接出现了大模型 API Key 和数据库密码，所以如果你后面要做“输入输出日志”，一定要顺手考虑：

- prompt / response 脱敏
- 生产环境按场景开关日志
- 长文本截断
- 敏感字段掩码

否则日志系统本身会变成新的泄露面。

如果你愿意，我下一步可以直接基于你当前项目结构，给你输出一版**“最小改造代码草案”**，包括：
- `AiGatewayService` 应该怎么改
- `LlmCallLog` 实体怎么设计
- 流式接口怎么拿 usage
- `application.yml` 该加哪些配置

_**User**_

系统在使用RAG的时候为什么是使用现在这种方式，Spring ai 不是提供了QuestionAnswerAdvisor吗，那种方式更好

_**Agent (gpt-5.4)**_

你现在这套 RAG，本质上是**“手工 RAG”**：

1. 先自己做向量检索。
2. 再把命中的片段拼成一个上下文字符串。
3. 最后把这个字符串作为普通 prompt 的一部分发给模型。

链路很清楚：

```64:80:src/main/java/com/my/ai/cursor/application/service/knowledge/KnowledgeSearchService.java
public String buildContext(String query, int topK) {
    List<KnowledgeSearchHit> hits = search(new KnowledgeSearchRequest(query, topK, null));
    if (hits.isEmpty()) {
        return "";
    }
    StringBuilder context = new StringBuilder();
    for (int i = 0; i < hits.size(); i++) {
        KnowledgeSearchHit hit = hits.get(i);
        context.append("片段").append(i + 1).append("：")
            .append(hit.title() == null ? "unknown" : hit.title())
            .append('\n');
        if (StringUtils.hasText(hit.sourceUrl())) {
            context.append("来源URL: ").append(hit.sourceUrl()).append('\n');
        }
        context.append(hit.content()).append("\n\n");
    }
    return context.toString();
}
```

然后在聊天接口里手工把上下文塞进 prompt：

```25:38:src/main/java/com/my/ai/cursor/application/service/chat/ChatService.java
public Flux<String> streamChatWithContext(String message, String context) {
    if (context == null || context.isBlank()) {
        return streamChat(message);
    }
    String ragPrompt = """
        你是一名医学知识问答助手，请优先基于给定资料回答，若资料不足请明确说明。

       【用户问题】
         %s
       【参考资料】
         %s
       """.formatted(message, context);

    return aiGatewayService.streamChat(AiScene.NORMAL_CHAT, ragPrompt);
}
```

所以你的问题其实是：**为什么不直接用 `QuestionAnswerAdvisor`，而是自己拼？**

## 为什么你现在会是这种写法

从代码看，这种写法不是随便写的，它有几个很现实的原因。

### 1. 你的检索结果不只是“给模型看”，还要“给业务系统看”

你有单独的知识检索服务和搜索接口：

```27:61:src/main/java/com/my/ai/cursor/application/service/knowledge/KnowledgeSearchService.java
public List<KnowledgeSearchHit> search(KnowledgeSearchRequest request) {
    // ...
    List<Document> docs = aiGatewayService.similaritySearch(builder.build());
    List<KnowledgeSearchHit> hits = new ArrayList<>();
    for (Document doc : docs) {
        Map<String, Object> metadata = doc.getMetadata();
        String vectorDocId = asString(metadata.get("vector_doc_id"));
        Double score = doc.getScore();
        if (StringUtils.hasText(vectorDocId)) {
            hits.add(knowledgeRepository.findHitByVectorDocId(vectorDocId, score, doc.getText()).orElse(
                new KnowledgeSearchHit(null, null, null, "unknown-source", null, null, null, doc.getText(), score,
                    vectorDocId)
            ));
        }
        else {
            hits.add(new KnowledgeSearchHit(
                null, null, null,
                asString(metadata.get("title")),
                asString(metadata.get("source_url")),
                asString(metadata.get("source_org")),
                asString(metadata.get("doc_type")),
                doc.getText(),
                score,
                null
            ));
        }
    }
    return hits;
}
```

这说明你这里的检索结果有业务属性：

- 要补 `title`
- 要补 `sourceUrl`
- 要补 `sourceOrg`
- 要补 `docType`
- 要补 `chunkId/documentId/chunkNo`

而且还专门通过 `vector_doc_id` 回表查业务库：

```92:104:src/main/java/com/my/ai/cursor/infrastructure/db/repository/KnowledgeRepository.java
public Optional<KnowledgeSearchHit> findHitByVectorDocId(String vectorDocId, Double score, String fallbackText) {
    Map<String, Object> row = kbDocumentChunkService.getBaseMapper().selectHitByVectorDocId(vectorDocId);
    if (row != null && !row.isEmpty()) {
        return Optional.of(new KnowledgeSearchHit(asLong(row.get("chunkId")), asLong(row.get("documentId")),
            asInteger(row.get("chunkNo")), asString(row.get("title")), asString(row.get("sourceUrl")),
            asString(row.get("sourceOrg")), asString(row.get("docType")), asString(row.get("content")), score,
            vectorDocId));
    }

    return Optional.of(
        new KnowledgeSearchHit(null, null, null, "unknown-source", null, null, null, fallbackText, score,
            vectorDocId));
}
```

这类场景下，自己先检索再加工，**业务可控性很高**。  
而 `QuestionAnswerAdvisor` 更偏向“检索完直接增强 prompt”，它对“给前端返回业务化命中列表”不是最直接的方案。

### 2. 你想完全控制最终 prompt 长什么样

你现在是显式写死了 RAG prompt 模板的：

```29:37:src/main/java/com/my/ai/cursor/application/service/chat/ChatService.java
String ragPrompt = """
    你是一名医学知识问答助手，请优先基于给定资料回答，若资料不足请明确说明。

   【用户问题】
     %s
   【参考资料】
     %s
   """.formatted(message, context);
```

这种做法的优点是非常直接：

- 上下文格式你说了算
- 标题、来源 URL 怎么展示你说了算
- 不满意就改字符串模板
- debug 很方便，最终 prompt 一眼可见

对刚做 RAG 的项目来说，这种方式常常是最先落地的。

### 3. 你当前系统其实不只是“聊天增强”，还包含“知识检索能力”本身

你有独立 `/api/kb/search`：

```52:55:src/main/java/com/my/ai/cursor/interfaces/KnowledgeController.java
@PostMapping("/search")
public List<KnowledgeSearchHit> search(@RequestBody KnowledgeSearchRequest request) {
    return knowledgeSearchService.search(request);
}
```

这意味着你的知识库检索本身就是一个独立能力，而不是纯粹为聊天服务。  
既然检索是独立能力，那么把“检索”和“生成”拆开，架构上也说得通。

## `QuestionAnswerAdvisor` 的优势是什么

如果换成 `QuestionAnswerAdvisor`，思路会变成：

- 仍然用 `VectorStore` 检索
- 但由 Advisor 自动把检索结果注入 prompt
- 业务代码只需要 `.prompt().advisors(...).user(message).call()`

它更像“**标准化 RAG 管道**”。

它的主要优点有这些。

### 1. 更符合 Spring AI 的设计方式

你现在的聊天调用已经是 `ChatClient` 风格了，但还没用 Advisor 链。  
如果接 `QuestionAnswerAdvisor`，RAG 会和：

- Chat Memory
- Logging Advisor
- 后续 Retrieval Augmentation
- 其它 Advisor

形成统一链路。长期演进会更顺。

### 2. 少写手工拼接代码

你现在要维护：

- 检索
- 命中结果映射
- 上下文字符串格式
- 聊天 prompt 拼装

如果纯聊天场景用 `QuestionAnswerAdvisor`，很多机械代码会减少。

### 3. 更适合“对话式 RAG”

尤其是以后你要加：

- 多轮对话记忆
- 按会话上下文改进检索
- 不同 Advisor 组合执行顺序

那 Advisor 模式会更自然。

## 但 `QuestionAnswerAdvisor` 不是一定更好

这里要分场景。

### 对你当前项目来说，现有方式有明显优点

你的代码里，知识命中结果是有业务含义的，不只是文档文本本身。文档入库时也明确写入了丰富 metadata：

```72:81:src/main/java/com/my/ai/cursor/application/service/ingestion/persistence/IngestionPersistenceService.java
private Map<String, Object> buildMetadata(Long documentId, PreparedChunkDto chunk, IngestionContext context) {
    Map<String, Object> metadata = new LinkedHashMap<>();
    metadata.put("vector_doc_id", chunk.vectorDocId());
    metadata.put("document_id", documentId);
    metadata.put("chunk_no", chunk.chunkNo());
    metadata.put("source_org", context.getRequest().sourceOrg());
    metadata.put("doc_type", context.getRequest().docType());
    metadata.put("title", context.getTitle());
    metadata.put("source_url", context.getRequest().sourceUrl());
    return metadata;
}
```

所以你现在不是简单的“搜一段文本给模型看”，而是：

- 检索结果要能单独返回
- 结果要带业务元信息
- 需要自己控制 how to cite / how to assemble context
- 以后可能还要加过滤条件、来源选择、文档类型限制

这种情况下，**手工 RAG 并不落后，反而更符合业务化知识库系统**。

### 现有方式的缺点也很明显

不过它也有几个问题：

- 聊天侧和检索侧耦合得比较松，靠字符串拼接，不够“框架化”
- 上下文拼接逻辑会越来越重
- 不利于后续叠加多种 Advisor
- 流式、日志、token、memory 等能力都要自己补
- prompt 很容易越来越臃肿

也就是说，它适合当前阶段，但**随着系统变复杂，维护成本会上升**。

## 哪种方式更好

我的判断是：

### 如果目标是“做一个业务可控的知识库系统”
你现在这种方式是合理的，而且短期内不一定比 `QuestionAnswerAdvisor` 差。

因为你更在意：

- 检索结果的业务结构化
- 对上下文格式的绝对控制
- 单独暴露检索接口
- 检索结果可解释、可展示

这几点，你当前方案反而更直接。

### 如果目标是“做一个标准的对话式 RAG 聊天系统”
`QuestionAnswerAdvisor` 一般更好。

因为它更适合：

- 聊天场景主导
- 后续接 `ChatMemory`
- 统一 Advisor 链
- 减少手工拼 prompt
- 框架升级时更容易跟着 Spring AI 走

## 对你项目最合适的建议

不是二选一，而是**混合用法**最好。

### 建议保留当前 `KnowledgeSearchService`
因为它承担的是“业务检索层”职责：

- 独立搜索接口
- 业务字段补全
- 命中结果展示
- 检索结果可解释

这层很有价值，不建议丢。

### 聊天入口可以逐步迁到 `QuestionAnswerAdvisor`
也就是：

- `/api/kb/search` 继续走你现在的检索服务
- `/api/chat/stream/rag` 这种对话式 RAG，改成 Advisor 风格

这样能同时拿到两边的好处：

- 检索层继续业务可控
- 聊天层更 Spring AI 化
- 后面接 memory / logger / observability 更顺

## 一句话结论

**你现在这种方式不是“没用上 Spring AI 的能力”，而是更偏“业务化手工 RAG”；`QuestionAnswerAdvisor` 则更偏“标准化对话 RAG”。**

对你当前这个项目现状，我会这样判断：

- 现在的写法：合理，尤其适合知识检索能力独立存在的系统
- 纯聊天场景：`QuestionAnswerAdvisor` 通常更优雅、更易扩展
- 最佳实践：保留当前检索服务，聊天接口逐步迁到 Advisor

如果你愿意，我可以下一步直接按你这个项目的结构，给你画一版“**当前手工 RAG** vs **改成 `QuestionAnswerAdvisor` 后的调用链对比**”，并顺带给你一版适合你项目的 `QuestionAnswerAdvisor` 示例代码。

Use the following context to answer the question.\n    CONTEXT:\n    {question_answer_context}\n    QUESTION:\n    {query}\n    \"\"\";\n\nvar ragAdvisor = QuestionAnswerAdvisor.builder(vectorStore)\n    .promptTemplate(PromptTemplate.builder().template(customTemplate).build())\n    .build();\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**注意事项:**\n*   此自定义模板配置仅影响 &#96;QuestionAnswerAdvisor&#96; 如何合并检索到的上下文与用户查询,与 &#96;ChatClient&#96; 级别的 &#96;templateRenderer&#96; 配置不同 [4]。\n*   默认情况下,Spring AI 使用基于 [StringTemplate](https://www.stringtemplate.org/) 的 &#96;StPromptTemplate&#96;,但你可以通过 &#96;.renderer()&#96; 方法自定义渲染器(例如修改起始和结束定界符)[4]。\n\nReferences:\n[1] https://mohankumarsagadevan.medium.com/spring-ai-101-building-a-rag-pipeline-the-questionansweradvisor-6950abe32f05\n    Title: Spring AI 101: Building a RAG Pipeline — The QuestionAnswerAdvisor | by Mohan Kumar Sagadevan | Jan, 2026 | Medium\n    Relevance: This page describes how to build a RAG pipeline using Spring AI, specifically focusing on the &#96;QuestionAnswerAdvisor&#96;.
